commands.review_summary:
The PR pre-creates an imports-disabled config fixture for one config-get fuzzy-match test and removes the checked-in v0.6.2 macOS release archive plus checksum from dist-bun.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, not from executing tests: a config with imports omitted uses DEFAULT_IMPORTS, so the fuzzy get test can see imported names outside its fixture. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor proof/normal maintainer review, not an automated code repair.

target.title: [codex] isolate config tests and remove legacy release archive
target.body:
## Summary

Isolates config CLI tests from the operator home data directory and removes the checked-in legacy `v0.6.2` macOS release archive now that the same archive and checksum exist on the GitHub release.

## Goal (Cherny pattern)
SUCCESS_CRITERION: MCPorter config CLI tests pass with isolated config/data homes and the redundant checked-in release archive is removed without losing release evidence.
VERIFY_COMMAND:    pnpm exec vitest run tests/cli-config-command.test.ts && pnpm run check
ROLLBACK_COMMAND:  git revert HEAD --no-edit
TIMEBOX:           PT2H

## Validation

- `gh release view v0.6.2 --repo openclaw/mcporter --json tagName,assets,url` confirmed `mcporter-macos-arm64-v0.6.2.tar.gz` and its `.sha256` asset exist on the release.
- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/cli-config-command.test.ts` passed: 22 tests passed.
- `pnpm run check` passed.

## Notes

Submitted from the `LDMB123/mcporter` fork because direct push to `openclaw/mcporter` is not permitted for this token.
target.author: ldmb123
